track_description: Exogenous new-vehicle efficiency improvement on the incumbent ICE fleet driven by turnover and tighter fuel-economy standards.
evidence_summary: The accepted passenger-road efficiency drift is an explicit decomposition of the existing ICE-fleet intensity decline already present in the library and consistent with Australian efficiency-standard and fleet-turnover evidence.
derivation_method: Hold the de-embedded ICE fleet fuel intensity flat in method_years.csv and move the previously implicit 2025-to-2050 decline into the autonomous track so attribution can refer to an explicit standards-driven drift.
double_counting_guardrail: Applies only to the ICE fleet after the matching decline is removed from the base row. Hybrid and BEV methods remain excluded because their efficiency is inseparable from drivetrain change.
review_notes: This is a calibration-first decomposition of incumbent ICE efficiency drift rather than a separate pathway transition or behavioural package.
